title: Working Women's Worst Enemy | #shorts
full_text: Women who don't need to work for a living. Women who have a lot of affluence and exposure to the world. Women who are protected by their husbands and brothers or dads or whatever their life circumstances. They feel free to judge me and say to me that I'm living in the past that India has moved on. No, they've moved on. That 1 2% is vacationing in Switzerland. That one 2% is wearing Gucci and Prada. My heart is with the everyday Indian woman. I work for a living. I openly talk about it. My husband lost his job during co. We're a family of five living in Manhattan. I run that household. >> My business supports many many people who work for me who have worked for me through since day one. So >> through co through the ups and downs I have paid everybody's salaries. I have paid everybody's invoices. That is a level of responsibility that I'm proud of >> and I relate to the working Indian woman who needs to make money. >> That's why I relate to you. >> Do you understand what I'm saying? But the women who are affluent and who like oh why is she presenting that India? Because that is my India. >> I have no desire to be part of your India. >> Yeah.
